items.title: Women say getting a long-acting reversible contraceptive has been the 'best decision'

children.content: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

items.title: Tugboat? Pipeline? Why there's no quick fuel fix for the Nuyina

children.content: Australia's Antarctic icebreaker can't access the marine fuel depot near its home port in Hobart. It currently makes a 674 kilometre detour to Burnie, but are there other options?

image.altText: Front on view of an icebreaker ship.

items.title: TasTAFE plans to cut 118 full-time jobs by 2030, documents show

children.content: Documents show TasTAFE plans to cut 118 full-time equivalent positions, including 63 teaching roles by 2030, and course fees could go up by 7 per cent.

image.altText: A TasTAFE campus signage in Hobart.

items.title: TassieCast 🔊 Boag’s calls last drinks in Launceston

children.content: Northern Tasmania has been rocked with the news that after 145 years — James Boag beer will no longer be produced in Launceston.

gallery.altText: a red brick building with grey skies and green signage

items.title: EPA clashes with Greens on need for real-time tracking of salmon antibiotic use

children.content: The director of Tasmania's Environment Protection Authority rejects calls to release real-time information on the amount of antibiotics used by salmon producers, despite the companies already providing that information to other government bodies.

image.altText: A woman with glasses leans forwards and speaks into a microphone in a committee room.

items.title: 'Major shake-up': Councillors circle top spot as lord mayor bows out

children.content: After eight years in the top role, Anna Reynolds has announced she will not seek re-election in October. Her departure coincides with a notable reduction in the number of council representatives.

image.altText: Anna Reynolds

items.title: Missing Tasmanian devil likely 'having the time of her life', wildlife expert says

children.content: A Tasmanian devil missing on the Gold Coast is likely "absolutely fine" and "having the time of her life", according to wildlife expert Greg Irons.

articles.title: No sign of missing Tasmanian devil Mary but experts confident she will survive

children.content: Sniffer dogs and thermal imaging drones are being deployed as the search for a missing Tasmanian devil on the Gold Coast goes into a second day.

items.title: This is the 17th Ebola outbreak in 50 years. But this one is different

children.content: Tensions are running high as officials fight to stop the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where aid workers tell the ABC they are already playing catch-up with the deadly disease.

items.title: Tantric workshops, couple swaps and toys: Intimacy pressures on MAFS exposed

image.altText: Woman sitting on a couch with a laptop on a coffee table in front of her.

children.content: Married At First Sight UK has become the centre of rape allegations. Now former MAFS Australia contestants and producers are speaking out about being coerced into intimacy.

items.title: Why war has made Iran's leadership frictions more 'consequential'

children.content: A report about the resignation of Iran's president earlier this week came at a turbulent moment for Iran's regime, which is grappling with frictions between moderates and hardliners as the war drags on.

articles.title: Trump slams bid to curb war powers, says Iran talks in 'final' phase

articles.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire as Israeli strikes kill four

children.content: Iran has made a ceasefire in Lebanon a condition for any peace deal with Washington, and has suggested in recent days that it could intervene directly if Israel keeps up attacks there.

items.title: The government is due to restore full fuel excise on June 30. Here's what to expect

items.title: Ancient tribes, military, trade and China's rise meet on this island

children.content: India's Great Nicobar Island is home to one of the world's last isolated tribes who are believed to have lived there for thousands of years, but a rising China is set to see it undergo a major transformation at the Indian government's insistence.

items.title: Teen's brutal death a flashpoint over violence against women and femicide

items.title: One Nation resurgence feels like 'recurring nightmare' for former Qld premier

items.title: Experts suspect state policy is putting some farmers in artificial drought
